message:
fix(scanner): three production bugs causing 0 deletions across fleet

Root cause: the scanner was running across 11 machines but never deleting
anything (0 deletions, 0 bytes freed despite thousands of scans). Three
independent bugs combined to make every candidate either score too low,
get age-vetoed, or get filtered by the open-file check.

Bug 1 — Directory size always 4KB:
  fs::Metadata::len() returns inode entry size (~4096) for directories,
  not content size. Added content_size_bytes field to EntryMetadata that
  accumulates immediate children's file sizes during walker iteration.
  Size factor went from 0.05 to 1.0 for multi-GB build targets.

Bug 2 — Active directories always age-vetoed:
  Directory mtime updates on every child add/remove, so target/ dirs on
  active build machines appeared perpetually young (<30min). Added
  effective_age_timestamp() that uses birth time (statx crtime) for
  directories, falling back to mtime. Birth time is stable across builds.

Bug 3 — Recursive open-file check too aggressive:
  OpenPathCache::is_path_open() recursively walked up to 20K entries in
  candidate subtrees, vetoing entire directories if ANY file was open.
  On active machines, target/debug/ always had open files. Switched all
  sites (CLI scan, clean, emergency, deletion executor) to lightweight
  collect_open_path_ancestors() + is_path_open_by_ancestor(), matching
  the daemon's existing approach.

Additionally:
- Policy engine default changed from Observe to Enforce (Observe mode
  blocked all deletions, defeating the purpose of the system)
- Policy engine wired into executor thread with proper gating
- DaemonState.policy_mode field added for status reporting
- All test constructors updated for new policy_mode field

Verified on CSD: disk went from 88% to 39% (449GB freed) within minutes.
Deployed to all 11 fleet machines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
